title: No edge-write Pi tool — in-pi agents cannot create DEC-0013 relations through the extension tool surface
description: For any harness-confined in-pi agent (workflow step, monitor, dispatched agent, or a tools-only Pi on a fresh install) restricted to extension-provided tools, there is no registered Pi tool to create a DEC-0013 relation (edge). relations.json is a bare top-level JSON array of {parent, child, relation_type, ordinal?}; the generic append-block-item / update-block-item tools target data[arrayKey] and therefore cannot append to a bare array (empirically: appendToBlock against relations throws 'must be array'). Edge creation exists only as the library writeRelations (project-context.ts) plus the orchestrator CLI (scripts/orchestrator/migrate-canonical-id.ts) plus the rename engine internals — none exposed as a Pi tool. So the foundational primitive of the whole model (an inter-item relationship) is unreachable through the tool surface. Intended outcome: an edge-write Pi tool, the dual-surface twin of writeRelations.

evidence.reference: tool enumeration: relation tools are all read-only (project-validate-relations, project-edges-for-lens, project-walk-descendants, walk-ancestors, find-references); no edge-write / append-relation tool is registered
writeRelations exists as a library surface (consumed by the rename-canonical-id engine + orchestrator migrate-canonical-id.ts) but has no pi.registerTool twin

relations is a bare top-level JSON array (no array_key), so generic append/update-block-item (which operate on object[arrayKey]) structurally cannot write edges; empirical probe: appendToBlock on relations fails 'must be array'
DEC-0013 (edges are THE inter-item relationship surface; FK-as-field forbidden) + DEC-0019/0020 (every substrate op = library + Pi tool + CLI script as a unit) — the Pi-tool leg is the missing one

impact: In-pi agents cannot author relationships: the .context re-population (DEC-0036 step 3), any tool-only consumer, and any in-pi workflow/monitor that needs to relate items are all blocked from the core primitive. The DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface is broken precisely at DEC-0013, the model's foundation. Sharpest of the three write-twin gaps — not even a degenerate path exists (append-block-item structurally rejects the bare-array shape; only write-block whole-file replace could blind-overwrite relations.json, unvalidated).
proposed_resolution: Register an edge-write Pi tool as the dual-surface twin of writeRelations — append a single edge {parent, child, relation_type, ordinal?} (and/or a bulk variant), routed through the canonical write path with the same atomicity (tmp+rename) and DispatchContext attestation as the item-CRUD tools. Guard semantics (relation_type registered in config.relation_types; endpoints resolve via buildIdIndex; cycle detection) either enforced at write or deferred to validateProject — implementer to choose per existing precedent (validateRelations already centralizes these checks). Implementation specifics outcome-only; survey the pi-sdk surface + sibling pi extensions for the canonical tool shape. DEC-0013 + DEC-0019/0020.
